title: Morning Robotics Scan
report:
## RoboTTT: Context Scaling for Robot Policies

### The Breakthrough
RoboTTT embeds test‑time training into vision‑language‑action models, turning the recurrent state into fast‑weight parameters that are updated by gradient descent during both training and inference. This enables conditioning on up to 8,000 timesteps of visual history—three orders of magnitude longer than prior policies—while keeping inference speed constant. The method yields an 87% performance gain over a single‑step baseline and a 62% boost versus a 1K‑timestep model on real‑robot assembly tasks.

### The Caveats
The paper never measures inference latency or computational cost, so its claim of constant‑speed inference despite 8 K‑step context is unsubstantiated.

## SUFLECA: Scaling Up Feature Learning for CAD‑to‑image Alignment

### The Breakthrough
SUFLECA learns geometry‑aware features from 674 K CAD‑image pairs using Normalized Object Coordinates to supervise pretrained visual models, producing compact, domain‑robust descriptors. A geometrically consistent matching algorithm then delivers zero‑shot 9‑DoF pose estimation in sub‑second time, achieving 33.4%/42.3% category/instance accuracy on ScanNet25k—a 10.3/12.2‑point gain over the strongest zero‑shot baseline and surpassing fully supervised methods.

### The Caveats
The paper reports gains over “zero‑shot and fully‑supervised baselines” without identifying which methods were used, preventing independent verification.

## Scaling Behavior Foundation Model for Humanoid Robots

### The Breakthrough
The authors introduce a Humanoid Transformer that treats diverse humanoid tasks as whole‑body motion tracking in a global frame, scaling on‑policy rollout volume with motion diversity. This yields over 10% MPKPE reduction locally and an 82% global improvement versus prior controllers, demonstrated on both simulation and real‑robot deployments.

### The Caveats
No comparison baselines are named, so it is unclear whether the reported improvements exceed existing humanoid controllers.

## AHEAD: Anticipatory Hand‑Driven Teleoperation via Human Intent Prediction

### The Breakthrough
AHEAD fuses 3D hand, head, and scene signals with an attention‑based classifier to predict intended grasp objects and placement slots, achieving 76% top‑1 accuracy. A state‑machine layer translates predictions into early robot goals, reducing object‑selection latency by 0.6 s and slot‑selection latency by 1.4 s compared to baseline teleoperation.

### The Caveats
The paper does not describe any baseline teleoperation methods, so the claimed latency improvements cannot be independently verified.

## PixEagle: vision‑based autonomy and tracking suite for PX4 drones and ground vehicles

### The Breakthrough
PixEagle provides a modular Python pipeline that combines five trackers (e.g., CSRT, YOLO) with active follower controllers via a REST‑API/WebSocket interface to PX4, enabling real‑time visual tracking and autonomous following on embedded Linux platforms, with optional GPU‑accelerated detection.

### The Caveats
Only informal demo videos are shown; no benchmark or baseline comparison is provided, leaving performance unquantified.

## Hyundai workers strike over planned deployment of Atlas humanoid robots

### The Breakthrough
Hyundai intends to deploy Boston Dynamics’ Atlas humanoid robots—6‑ft tall, 100‑lb lifting units—at scale, targeting more than 25,000 units across Hyundai and Kia plants, each costing $130,000 and projected to recoup costs within about two years.

### The Caveats
The cost‑effectiveness and large‑scale deployment claim lack real‑world performance, safety, or cost‑impact data.

## Moonshot AI unveils Kimi K3 2.8 T LLM and revisits the pelican benchmark

### The Breakthrough
Kimi K3 doubles its predecessor’s parameters to 2.8 T and introduces a single “max” reasoning mode that consumes ~13 k reasoning tokens for a 95‑token prompt, costing roughly $0.25, while supporting image input and showing strong private leaderboard scores.

### The Caveats
The edge is based only on private leaderboard scores and an informal SVG demo, with no public benchmark or robotics evaluation.

## Show HN: Favur Evals – multi‑agent code‑generation harness with eight‑metric scoring

### The Breakthrough
Favur Evals orchestrates 14 specialized agents (planner, coder, tester, reviewer, etc.) under a central controller and automatically scores end‑to‑end runs on eight engineering criteria, recording full execution traces for replay and per‑seat behavior fingerprinting.

### The Caveats
Rankings derive from a single fixed statement of work using internal model variants, lacking comparison to external baselines.

## Lific: lightweight self‑hosted issue tracker for AI coding agents

### The Breakthrough
Lific packs a full‑featured issue‑tracking UI, authentication, status, blockers, and comment threads into a single Rust binary with an integrated MCP server, allowing AI agents to query and update issues via natural‑language commands stored in a SQLite file.

### The Caveats
Performance claims (handling hundreds of issues) are unsubstantiated, with no benchmarks or user studies.

### What to watch
RoboTTT and SUFLECA both push perception‑driven robotics forward: RoboTTT expands temporal context for manipulation policies, while SUFLECA improves zero‑shot 9‑DoF pose estimation from CAD‑image pairs. Watching how long‑context conditioning and geometry‑aware features converge could shape next‑gen embodied AI pipelines.

### Priority
RoboTTT’s ability to condition policies on 8 K timesteps without slowing inference promises immediate gains for long‑horizon manipulation, making it the most actionable breakthrough today.

### One action
Check the RoboTTT paper and repository to experiment with extended‑context policies on your own robot manipulation tasks.
